basic_info.name: Lin Xiaoyang
basic_info.occupation: UX Researcher at a Major Internet Company

travel_style: Semi-independent travel (self-booked long-haul transport, local group tours or customized itineraries)
travel_background.annual_trips: One long-haul international trip per year (10-14 days) + one domestic long-distance trip (7 days) + 4-5 weekend getaways within the Jiangsu-Zhejiang-Shanghai region

family.name: Lin Jianguo
family.travel_connection: A retired middle school geography teacher with a passion for collecting maps and train schedules. He serves as Lin Xiaoyang's 'historical consultant'; before every trip to Europe, she discusses the local historical context with him. Due to work constraints in his youth, he only visited Beijing and Shanghai. His biggest wish now is to take the Qinghai-Tibet Railway, and Xiaoyang has promised to accompany him next year.

family.name: Chen Xiulan
family.travel_connection: A typical Chinese mother who worries about safety and hygiene, initially opposing her daughter's solo long-distance travels. However, after Xiaoyang took her to Singapore (clean, safe, and Chinese-speaking), her attitude shifted dramatically. She now acts as Xiaoyang's 'logistics minister,' stuffing a backpack full of cold medicine and pickled vegetables before every trip.

family.name: Lin Yue
family.travel_connection: A college student born after 2000 who started traveling under Xiaoyang's influence. They once traveled together to Thailand, but their styles clashed (Xiaoyang wanted to visit museums, while her cousin only wanted to check in at influencer spots). After that trip, Xiaoyang decided to seek peers or travel solo for future long-distance journeys.

social_circle.name: Zhang Wei
social_circle.travel_connection: Their personalities complement each other: Zhang Wei excels at creating Excel itineraries and comparing prices, while Xiaoyang is responsible for uncovering hidden gems and taking photos. They make a perfect team and have jointly visited Kansai (Japan), Italy, and Switzerland. She is Xiaoyang's most reliable long-distance travel companion.

social_circle.name: Ah Jie
social_circle.travel_connection: An outdoor enthusiast and programmer. He once invited Xiaoyang to hike the Milford Track in New Zealand. That experience made Xiaoyang fall in love with natural scenery but also realize her physical limits; she is not suited for hardcore outdoor activities and prefers 'comfortable hiking' instead.

social_circle.name: Lao K
social_circle.travel_connection: A professional photographer from Shanghai whom she met on an Aurora tour in Iceland. Although they haven't traveled long-distance together, they frequently exchange tips on photography locations and camera settings. Lao K has recommended many obscure shooting spots to Xiaoyang.

daily_habits.time_pattern: During commute on the subway (40 minutes each way, morning and evening)
daily_habits.activity: Browsing Xiaohongshu, Instagram, or Mafengwo to save images of potential travel destinations and organize inspiration boards.
Learning basic conversation in a new language (currently studying basic Italian and reviewing Japanese) using Duolingo or by watching TV dramas.

daily_habits.time_pattern: The last weekend of every month
daily_habits.activity: Organizing photos taken during the month, color grading and archiving them, selecting nine to post on WeChat Moments, or writing a short travel blog post.
Monitoring flight price fluctuations, following official accounts for discounted airfares, and budget planning for the next trip.

emotion_travel_map.behavior: Tends to visit seaside locations or quiet ancient towns (e.g., Dali, Kamakura). Prefers no dense itinerary; just wants to zone out, drink coffee, and listen to the waves, healing through 'slowness'.
Chooses cities rich in culture and history (e.g., Kyoto, Rome, Xi'an). Visits museums, churches, or ancient sites, seeking direction through dialogue with history.

emotion_travel_map.emotion: Excitement / Celebration (e.g., promotion, completing a major project)
emotion_travel_map.behavior: Selects challenging or scenically magnificent places (e.g., Iceland, New Zealand, Western Sichuan). Tries thrilling activities like skydiving or glacier hiking to release energy.
Chooses places with rich cuisine and a vibrant atmosphere (e.g., Chengdu, Changsha, Penang). Finds comfort through satisfying flavors and the bustling crowds of night markets.

life_events.time: 2018 (Just before graduating with a master's degree)
life_events.event: Completed her first solo international trip—a 10-day solo journey through Kansai, Japan—funded by scholarships and part-time income. This experience taught her how to handle emergencies alone (such as missing a train) and established her confidence in 'independent travel'.

life_events.time: 2021 (Third year of work, encountering a career bottleneck)
life_events.event: Resigned and took a two-month gap, driving solo around Iceland. Amidst extreme solitude and magnificent nature, she re-evaluated her career path. Upon returning, she decided to transition from product operations to user research, finding a career direction she is more passionate about.

Took her parents abroad for the first time, meticulously planning a 5-day trip to Singapore. Seeing her parents as happy as children at Universal Studios, she deeply realized that travel is not just about self-exploration but also a bond connecting family. Since then, she has included 'family-friendly' considerations in her travel planning.

preferences.accommodation: Prefers boutique homestays with unique design or convenient 3-star+ hotels. Rejects noisy dormitory rooms in hostels but enjoys hostel lobbies with public social spaces. Places high importance on hygiene and bedding quality.
preferences.transport: Prefers flying for long distances (window seat preferred). Favors trains for travel within Europe and Japan to enjoy the scenery. Prefers walking or public transport within cities. Occasionally rents a car for self-driving but avoids driving at night.
preferences.food: Rejects tourist group meals. Enjoys hunting for food at local eateries ('fly restaurants'), morning markets, or night markets where locals gather. Knowledgeable about coffee and desserts; must visit 2-3 specialty cafes in every city.
preferences.photography: Gear: Sony A7C II + 20-70mm lens + DJI Pocket 3. Style: Prefers natural light, capturing street human moments and grand landscapes. Dislikes overly retouched portraits.
preferences.souvenirs: Does not buy mass-produced trinkets. Only collects fridge magnets (one per city), locally characteristic postcards (sent to herself or friends), and practical tableware or textile items.
